summary: Surah Al-Mulk proclaims Allah’s absolute sovereignty, the creation of death and life as a test, and the perfection of the heavens adorned with lamps that repel devils. It depicts the fate of deniers in Hell and praises those who fear their Lord unseen. It calls to reflect on birds, sustenance, and human helplessness without Allah, ending with the challenge: if your water vanished, who could bring it back?
